notes: Three back-to-back no-op turns ('Reply with exactly the word: ok') in ONE live vibe REPL session, locking down the multi-turn TOKEN ACCUMULATOR. KNOWN FAILING (daemon=bug): the vibe parser.go applySidecar emits ONLY a TokenSnapshot{Total: stats.context_tokens} on turn_done (sidecar.go reads no other stats field), so there is NO PerTurnContribution/UsageBreakdown and NO cost — the monotonic SessionMetrics.TotalTokens accumulation and the cache-bucket distinction the scenario's acceptance requires never reach events.jsonl. The data EXISTS in meta.json.stats (session_total_llm_tokens, last_turn_prompt_tokens, last_turn_completion_tokens, session_cost, input/output_price_per_million); a feasible live-tailing fix reads last_turn_prompt/completion_tokens + prices into a per-turn contribution — a known cost/accumulation follow-up. context_tokens IS emitted (tokens_nonzero=true) but it is a context-window snapshot, not the accumulator, and can shrink on compaction. cost_nonzero=false (never emitted; mistral-vibe also absent from core/pkg/capacity). Cache-bucket sub-assertion (CacheReadTokens>0, fresh input=0) is agent-side unavailable — Vibe's stats expose no cache_read field, so it is unobservable regardless of a parser fix. The phase DSL asserts only the three ready→working→ready cycles on ONE session that the accounting rides on (the accumulation claim has no metric form in the phase DSL). Single-birth adapter (session id = the session_<...> transcript directory, sessionIDFromPath), modeled like claudecode/basic-turn: birth anchored to start unpinned; every turn phase pins to the birth session via same_session_as so a co-occurring proc-<PID> presession can never satisfy them. Recipe needs wait_turn, stubbed by the scaffolded driver (driver=gap:wait_turn); record ports it before driving. Model mistral-medium-3.5 confirmed from live config.active_model.

text: session_<...> session born in ready when the transcript directory appears (single-birth; id from the transcript directory)
vibe PID bound to the session (matched by cwd)
Turn 1 text-only assistant reply (turn_done) → ready; turn_done carries Tokens.Total>0. KNOWN FAILING: no PerTurnContribution/cost, so TotalTokens>0 via the accumulator is not surfaced.
Turn 2 completes back to ready; correct behavior: TotalTokens after T2 ≥ TotalTokens after T1 (monotonic). KNOWN FAILING: no accumulator emitted.
Turn 3 completes back to ready; correct behavior: TotalTokens after T3 ≥ TotalTokens after T2 (monotonic accumulation across 3 turns). KNOWN FAILING: no accumulator emitted.
